title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Authentic Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani) - Easy Steps
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful – perfect for an Indian lunch. Get the best homemade butter chicken!

seo.imageAlt: A bowl of creamy, rich authentic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro, ready to serve with naan.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich and creamy flavors of homemade Butter Chicken, also known as Murgh Makhani, a classic Indian delight.

description: Experience the rich, creamy indulgence of Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani, a beloved Indian classic. This dish features tender chicken simmered in a luscious tomato-based gravy, enriched with butter, cream, and aromatic spices. Perfect for a satisfying lunch, it promises a burst of authentic Indian flavors in every bite.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast

ingredients.notes: soaked in hot water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix well to coat the chicken. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook until browned on all sides and mostly cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add 2 tablespoons of butter. Once melted, add chopped onions and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining ginger-garlic paste, green cardamoms, cinnamon stick, and bay leaf. Saute for 1 minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Gravy
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato puree, soaked cashews (drained), and a pinch of salt. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat, cover, and cook for 10-12 minutes, allowing the tomatoes to break down and the flavors to meld.

instructions.title: Blend the Gravy
instructions.text: Remove the bay leaf, cinnamon stick, and cardamom pods (if you can find them easily). Carefully transfer the mixture to a blender or use an immersion blender. Blend until completely smooth. If too thick, add a little water to reach desired consistency. Pass the gravy through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan for an extra smooth texture, discarding any solids.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the pan with the smooth gravy to medium-low heat. Add the cooked chicken pieces, the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter, crushed fenugreek leaves, and sugar. Stir well and simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the flavors.

instructions.title: Add Cream and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the heavy cream and cook for just 1-2 minutes more, ensuring the cream does not curdle. Do not boil after adding cream. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro and serve hot with naan, roti, or rice.

tips: For the smoothest gravy, ensure you blend the tomato-cashew mixture thoroughly and then pass it through a fine-mesh sieve.
To achieve a vibrant red color without artificial food coloring, use good quality Kashmiri red chili powder which provides color rather than intense heat.
Always add the heavy cream at the very end and do not boil the gravy after adding it, as this can cause the cream to curdle. Simmer gently for just a minute or two.
article:
Unveiling the unparalleled delight of Indian cuisine, one dish stands out for its lusciousness, vibrant color, and comforting aroma: Butter Chicken, also known as Murgh Makhani. This iconic Indian lunch recipe has captured hearts worldwide, becoming a staple in Indian restaurants and a beloved comfort food in homes across the globe. Its rich, creamy tomato-based sauce, infused with aromatic spices and surrounding tender pieces of chicken, offers an exquisite balance of sweet and savory, with just a hint of tang. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a culinary novice, mastering Butter Chicken promises a rewarding experience and a truly unforgettable meal. Let's dive into the world of this culinary masterpiece.

## What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?

Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, literally meaning "chicken with butter," is a classic Indian curry that originated in Delhi, India, in the mid-20th century. It’s widely credited to Kundan Lal Gujral, one of the founders of Moti Mahal restaurant. The story goes that he created the dish by simmering leftover tandoori chicken in a rich, buttery tomato gravy, giving birth to what would become one of India's most famous exports.

At its core, Butter Chicken is characterized by its velvety smooth, bright orange-red sauce. This luxurious gravy is typically made from tomatoes, butter (lots of it!), fresh cream, and a blend of spices such as garam masala, ginger, garlic, and fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi). The chicken, often marinated in yogurt and spices and then grilled or pan-fried, is incredibly tender and succulent, soaking up all the flavors of the rich sauce. It’s a dish that embodies indulgence, offering a mild, approachable flavor profile that appeals to nearly every palate, making it a perfect introduction to Indian cuisine for newcomers and a cherished favorite for connoisseurs. As an Indian lunch recipe, it's hearty yet not overly heavy, providing sustained energy for the rest of the day.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting authentic Butter Chicken requires a few key components. Here’s a breakdown of what you’ll need for both the chicken and the sumptuous sauce:

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 tsp red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color, less heat)
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1/2 tsp garam masala
*   Salt to taste

For the Murgh Makhani Sauce:
*   2 tbsp unsalted butter (and more for finishing)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 tsp red chili powder
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1 tsp garam masala
*   1/2 tsp ground cumin
*   1/2 tsp ground coriander
*   1 (400g) can crushed tomatoes or 3-4 fresh large tomatoes, pureed
*   1/2 cup water or chicken broth
*   1/4 cup cashews, soaked in hot water for 15 minutes (optional, for creaminess)
*   1 tbsp kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   1/4 cup heavy cream (plus more for garnish)
*   1 tsp sugar (or to taste, balances acidity)
*   Salt to taste
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped, for garnish

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps to create your own delectable Butter Chicken:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all marinade ingredients. Mix well, cover, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours.
2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat a drizzle of oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken in batches, cooking until lightly charred and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per batch. Set aside.
3.  **Prepare the Sauce Base:** In the same pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes.
4.  **Add Aromatics and Spices:** Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Add the red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, cumin, and coriander. Sauté for 30 seconds, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
5.  **Simmer the Tomatoes:** Pour in the crushed tomatoes and water (or broth). Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly.
6.  **Blend for Smoothness:** If using cashews, drain them and add to the sauce. Carefully transfer the sauce to a blender or use an immersion blender to process until completely smooth. Pass the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve for an extra silky texture, pressing down on solids.
7.  **Combine and Finish:** Return the smooth sauce to the pan. Add the cooked chicken, kasuri methi, sugar, and salt. Stir well and simmer for another 5-10 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the sauce's flavors.
8.  **Enrich with Cream and Butter:** Stir in the heavy cream and a knob of fresh butter. Cook for just 1-2 minutes more, ensuring the sauce doesn't boil after adding cream. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
9.  **Garnish and Serve:** Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro and an extra drizzle of cream.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving restaurant-quality Butter Chicken at home is entirely possible with a few expert tricks:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marinating the chicken. The yogurt helps tenderize the meat, while the spices infuse it with flavor, creating that signature taste even before it hits the sauce. Overnight marination is ideal.
*   **Char the Chicken:** For an authentic taste, try to get a slight char on your chicken pieces. This mimics the tandoor oven effect and adds a smoky depth that elevates the final dish. A cast-iron skillet works wonders for this.
*   **Smooth Sauce is Paramount:** Blending the sauce until absolutely smooth, and even straining it, is crucial for that luxurious, velvety texture that defines Murgh Makhani. Don't rush this step. If using fresh tomatoes, blanch and peel them before pureeing for an even silkier result.
*   **Balance of Flavors:** The beauty of Butter Chicken lies in its delicate balance. A touch of sugar helps cut through the acidity of the tomatoes, while kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves) adds a unique, slightly bitter, and aromatic complexity that is essential. Don't skip the kasuri methi!
*   **Quality Ingredients:** Use good quality canned crushed tomatoes or ripe, fresh tomatoes. Fresh ginger-garlic paste makes a noticeable difference compared to pre-made versions. And, of course, good quality butter and cream are non-negotiable for the richness.
*   **Don't Boil After Adding Cream:** Once you've added the heavy cream, only gently simmer the sauce for a minute or two. Boiling cream can cause it to curdle, affecting the texture.

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of accompaniments, making it a truly satisfying Indian lunch recipe.

*   **Naan Bread:** This is arguably the most classic pairing. The soft, fluffy naan is perfect for scooping up every last bit of that rich, creamy sauce. Garlic naan or butter naan takes it to another level.
*   **Basmati Rice:** Steamed basmati rice, with its fragrant, long grains, offers a beautiful contrast to the richness of the curry and helps absorb the flavors.
*   **Jeera Rice:** Rice cooked with cumin seeds (jeera) adds an extra layer of aroma and subtle spice.
*   **Raita:** A cooling yogurt-based side dish, often with cucumber or onion, helps to balance the richness of the Butter Chicken and cleanse the palate.
*   **Fresh Salad:** A simple side salad of sliced onions, cucumbers, and tomatoes with a squeeze of lemon can add a refreshing crunch.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken recipe is perfect as is, there are always ways to adapt it to your preferences or dietary needs.

*   **Vegetarian Butter "Chicken":** Substitute the chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), tofu, or mixed vegetables like cauliflower and peas. Marinate and cook them similarly before adding to the sauce.
*   **Spice Level:** For more heat, increase the amount of red chili powder or add a finely chopped green chili during the onion sautéing stage. For less heat, reduce the chili powder or use sweet paprika for color instead.
*   **Dairy-Free:** For a dairy-free version, use coconut cream instead of heavy cream and a plant-based butter alternative. Note that this will alter the flavor profile slightly, giving it a subtle coconut undertone.
*   **Nut-Free:** If you have a nut allergy, omit the cashews. The sauce will still be delicious, though perhaps a little less thick. You can use a small amount of roasted sunflower seeds or melon seeds (char magaz) instead if desired, blended thoroughly.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** For an extra smoky kick, you can use a small piece of charcoal. Heat it until red hot, place it in a small metal bowl within the curry, drizzle a little oil or ghee over it, and quickly cover the pot for a few minutes. This infuses the Butter Chicken with a traditional dhungar (smoking) technique.

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is more than just a meal; it's an experience. Its creamy texture, depth of flavor, and comforting warmth make it an ideal choice for a satisfying lunch or a special dinner. By following these guidelines and tips, you're well on your way to creating a truly spectacular dish that will impress and delight. Enjoy the journey of making and savoring this magnificent Indian culinary treasure!
